old_claim.note: Naval Logbooks provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because the scoped node is a Custom House ship-registration record, while voyage logbooks record courses, weather, observations, or voyage particulars rather than vessel ownership registry.

source_shape.source_locator: The registration guide says shipping registers generally do not show voyage, cargo, or crew details except masters in earlier registers.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source separates registry contents from voyage/logbook-style details.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: That separation makes naval logbooks adjacent maritime records, not a direct prerequisite for the scoped Custom House registration practice.

ontology_before: Merchant Marine Registry depended directly on Naval Logbooks, implying voyage logs enabled official ship registration.
ontology_after: Merchant Ship Registration depends directly on port customs recordkeeping; voyage logbooks are outside the corrected registry scope.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: merchant_marine_registry no longer directly depends on naval_logbooks.
would_reject_if: The node were broadened to include voyage logging and operational sea records.
A stronger source showed Custom House ship registers were built from naval logbooks.
The naval_logbooks node were narrowed to mean official ship identity registers rather than voyage logs.
short_reason: Ship registration records are not voyage logbooks.
